one_line_takeaway: MAPA shows that alternating text-only, unconnected text+image, and connected text+image prompt variants within each turn (chosen via a semantic-correlation greedy search) plus SEM-driven cross-turn Advance/Regen/Back trajectory adjustment substantially outperforms prior single- and multi-turn jailbreak methods against safety-aligned LVLMs, because naively pairing malicious images with existing multi-turn LLM jailbreak prompts actually makes such attacks easier to defend against.
key_finding: MAPA achieves 96.66% average ASR on HarmBench across LLaVA/Qwen/Llama (98.33% / 100.00% / 91.66% respectively), beating the strongest multi-turn baseline CoA (70.55% average) by 26.11 points and outperforming all baselines by the paper's claimed 15-30% margin. On additional benchmarks MAPA reaches 91.11% average on JailbreakBench, 96.11% on AdvBench, and 93.89% on RedTeam-2K, exceeding the next-best baseline by 23.33, 14.45, and 32.22 points respectively. Against GPT-4o-mini, MAPA reaches 88.33% ASR under the default (Llama-2-13B) judge and 93.33% under an advanced (GPT-4o-mini) judge, versus 53.33%/73.33% for the strongest baseline (CoA). Naively combining vision with existing multi-turn LLM attacks can hurt performance: CoA+visual(SD) averaged 57.22% on HarmBench, below plain text-only CoA's 70.55%. Ablations show reflection contributes +8.89 points on average (88.89% full MAPA vs. 80.00% w/o reflection), cross-turn policy adjustment contributes +12.23 points (76.66% without it), and using any single attack action instead of alternating all three costs at least 7.78 points (worst case Attack Action 3 alone: 47.78%, a 32.22-point drop). Under a query-budget-matched comparison (Table 5), MAPA still outperforms multi-turn baselines while using comparable or fewer average queries (e.g., 98.33% ASR at ~17.88 queries on LLaVA vs. CoA's 83.33% at ~20.17 queries). Against defenses, MAPA's ASR drops by 19.45 points against AdaShield-Static and 13.89 points against Llama-Guard-3-Vision-11B but remains substantial (69.44% and 75.00% average respectively).
core_contribution: The paper proposes MAPA (Multi-turn Adaptive Prompting Attack), the first multi-turn text-vision jailbreak attack framework against large vision-language models (LVLMs). MAPA uses a two-level design: (1) within each turn, an Attacker LLM plus a Connector LLM generate three candidate 'attack actions' — unconnected text only, unconnected text + a Stable-Diffusion-generated malicious image, and connected (text-vision-aligned) text + malicious image — and a greedy search picks the action whose response has the highest semantic correlation (SEM, cosine similarity of encoded representations) with the malicious jailbreak task; (2) across turns, the attack trajectory is adjusted via three policies (Advance/Regen/Back) that compare SEM with and without historical dialogue context to decide whether to move to the next turn, regenerate the current turn's prompt, or revert to the previous turn. A reflection mechanism additionally allows up to 3 attempts per malicious task, feeding failed attack chains and victim responses back to the Attacker LLM to construct improved chains. The authors motivate the design with an empirical finding (Section 4.2/Figure 1) that naively adding malicious visual content to existing multi-turn LLM jailbreaks (e.g., attaching Stable-Diffusion or UltraBreak-generated images to Chain-of-Attack prompts) can make attacks easier for safety-aligned LVLMs to defend against, rather than easier to succeed.
threat_model: Black-box, structure-based jailbreak attack against safety-aligned LVLMs. The attacker has query access only (no gradients or model internals) to a victim LVLM (LLaVA-v1.6-Mistral-7B, Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct, Llama-3.2-Vision-11B-Instruct, and the commercial GPT-4o-mini) and uses an open-source red-teaming/attacker LLM (Mistral-Small-3-24B-Instruct-2501, chosen deliberately as a small non-SOTA model for reproducibility) plus a Stable Diffusion image generator to construct multi-turn text-and-image prompt sequences whose goal is to elicit a harmful, on-task response within a bounded number of turns (max 5 turns, max 10 iterations per turn, max 3 reflection attempts), as judged by a Judge LLM (the fine-tuned Llama-2-13B HarmBench judge, or GPT-4o-mini itself as an 'Advanced Judge' in one experiment).
limitations: The paper's own ablations show performance is not uniform across models/attack actions: for Llama-3.2-Vision-11B-Instruct specifically, text-only Attack Action 1 outperforms the vision-including actions because that model has comparatively stronger visual-modality safety filtering, meaning the value of the vision channel is model-dependent rather than universal. The greedy per-turn search over 3 attack actions inherently multiplies query cost versus single-action multi-turn baselines, which the authors address only via a budget-matched comparison rather than eliminating the cost. Evaluation relies on automated Judge LLMs (HarmBench's fine-tuned Llama-2-13B, or GPT-4o-mini as judge) and a SimCSE-style cosine-similarity SEM score, both of which are themselves imperfect proxies for true harm and could introduce judge-specific bias, though the paper does test sensitivity to judge choice (Table 3). Effectiveness against defended LVLMs, while still high, degrades notably (13.89-19.45 points) against two specific defenses (AdaShield-Static, Llama-Guard-3-Vision-11B), and only two defenses were tested. The attacker/connector LLM is fixed to a single mid-size open model (Mistral-Small-3-24B-Instruct-2501); the paper does not report how MAPA's effectiveness varies with different or stronger attacker LLMs. Benchmarks are evaluated on reduced/sampled subsets (60-100 tasks per benchmark) rather than full benchmark sets.

quantitative_results: HarmBench average ASR: MAPA 96.66% vs CoA 70.55% vs VRP 45.56% vs MML 28.33% vs ActorAttack 27.22% vs FootInTheDoor 58.89% vs CoA+visual(SD) 57.22% vs CoA+visual(UltraBreak) 67.11%
JailbreakBench average ASR: MAPA 91.11% vs CoA 67.78% vs ActorAttack 29.05% vs FootInTheDoor 55.00% vs VRP/MML 0.00%
AdvBench average ASR: MAPA 96.11% vs CoA 81.66% vs ActorAttack 31.75% vs FootInTheDoor 57.22%
RedTeam-2K average ASR: MAPA 93.89% vs CoA 61.67% vs ActorAttack 18.35% vs FootInTheDoor 49.44%
GPT-4o-mini ASR (Default Judge / Advanced Judge): MAPA 88.33%/93.33% vs CoA 53.33%/73.33% vs ActorAttack 45.76%/64.40% vs FootInTheDoor 41.66%/60.00%

Ablation (30-task HarmBench sample): full MAPA 88.89% avg; w/o Reflection 80.00% (-8.89); repeat-w/o-Reflection 84.44% (-4.45); w/o Policy Adjustment 76.66% (-12.23); Attack Action 1 only 72.22% (-7.78); Attack Action 2 only 62.22% (-17.78); Attack Action 3 only 47.78% (-32.22); text-only MAPA 82.22% (-6.67)
Budget-matched ASR (avg queries) on HarmBench sampled, LLaVA: MAPA 98.33%(#17.88) vs CoA 83.33%(#20.17) vs ActorAttack 65.00%(#22.47) vs FootInTheDoor 75.00%(#24.47)
Defended LVLM ASR (avg): AdaShield-Static 69.44% (-19.45 vs undefended), Llama-Guard-3-Vision-11B 75.00% (-13.89 vs undefended)
Average SEM successful vs failed jailbreaks (HarmBench sampled): Llama 0.6738 vs 0.5773; LLaVA 0.6740 vs 0.6572; Qwen 0.6735 vs 0.6490
